lines.en_lit: And if the second one was superior to it, he brings that one.

Rava said: the mishna is precise in accordance with of Rav, and the baraita in accordance with of Rabbi Yoḥanan. the mishna in accordance with of Rav, as it teaches: If to God died, that upon which the lot for God shall stand in its stead. And the other stands as it stood

the baraita in accordance with of Rabbi Yoḥanan, as it teaches: second I do not know whether the second of the first pair, or the second of the second pair. When states: “It shall be stood alive not whose counterpart died. From where may it be inferred, “It shall be stood alive,” not that already stood

We learned And furthermore, Rabbi Yehuda said: the blood spilled the scapegoat to die. if the scapegoat dies, the blood should be spilled, Granted, according to Rabbi Yoḥanan, who said animals are rejected, due to that the scapegoat to die. However, according to Rav, who said animals are not rejected, why is the scapegoat to die?
Rav said to you: I did not state in accordance with of Rabbi Yehuda. I stated in accordance with of the Rabbis.

Granted, according to of Rav, Rabbi Yehuda and the Rabbis disagree with regard to However, according to of Rabbi Yoḥanan, with regard to what do disagree? Rava said: Didn’t we say the mishna is precise in accordance with of Rav,

We learned that a communal sin-offering is not to die. This of an individual a case like this to die. Granted, according to of Rabbi Yoḥanan, in accordance with Rabbi Abba Rav said. As Rabbi Abba said that Rav said:
